businessSummary.oneLiner: Leading AI data analytics platform for government and commercial clients — powered by Gotham, Foundry, and AIP
businessSummary.description: Palantir provides data integration, analytics, and AI decision-making platforms for government and commercial organizations. FY2025 revenue $4.48B (+70% YoY), adjusted operating margin 57%. Wednesday close $150.14 (-0.66%). Small pullback after +15% weekly rally on Iran war tailwinds. Institutional ownership surged from 7% to 32% in one year. Market cap $359.1B, P/E ~238x, Forward P/E ~114x.
howTheyMakeMoney: Government contracts: Gotham/Apollo — defense, intelligence agencies, NATO (~55% of revenue)
Commercial contracts: Foundry/AIP — enterprise AI platform, Boot Camp strategy (~45% of revenue)
SaaS model: Annual subscriptions + expansion-based. Net Dollar Retention 120%+

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on EPS $0.63. Top-tier premium even within AI sector

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026E EPS $1.32. Still elevated but reflects growth

keyMetrics.name: Revenue Growth (YoY)
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025. Accelerating on AI demand explosion

keyMetrics.name: ROE
keyMetrics.interpretation: High return on equity. Capital-efficient growth

growthDrivers.title: Iran War Tailwind — +15% Weekly Rally, Wall Street 'Loves' PLTR
growthDrivers.description: US-Iran military conflict structurally expanding demand for Palantir's Gotham platform (battlefield AI, target identification). Barchart: 'Rare case of a software stock rallying on geopolitics.'

growthDrivers.title: Institutional Ownership Surges 7% → 32% — Structural Demand Shift
growthDrivers.description: GuruFocus analysis: Institutional ownership grew 4.6x in one year. Transitioning from retail-driven to institutional-driven. Contributes to price stability.

growthDrivers.title: Ontology Moat — The 'Tool Factory' for AI and Human Agents
growthDrivers.description: Palantir's Ontology platform goes beyond analytics to serve as a 'tool factory' where AI and human agents collaborate. Unique competitive edge in battle planning and target identification.

growthDrivers.title: +2,200% Since 2023 — Structural AI Supercycle
growthDrivers.description: Motley Fool analysis: +2,200% gain since 2023. AIP Boot Camp strategy driving rapid commercial customer acquisition with dual government+commercial growth.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 20+ year track record in government/defense AI. Security clearance serves as entry barrier. 'Ontology' platform provides a unique AI-human collaboration interface.

moats.description: Top-level US defense and intelligence security clearances. Competitors cannot enter.
'Tool factory' for AI and human agents. Unmatched in battle planning and target identification.
5-day intensive pilots convert to enterprise-wide deployments. High conversion rate.

competitors.name: Microsoft (Azure AI)
competitors.detail: Cloud AI platform. General-purpose vs Palantir's specialized analytics

competitors.name: Snowflake
competitors.detail: Data lakehouse. Some overlap in analytics domain

competitors.name: C3.ai
competitors.detail: Enterprise AI competitor. Smaller scale and profitability

competitors.name: Anduril
competitors.detail: Defense AI startup. Growing fast but much smaller than Palantir

recentNews.headline: Wall Street 'Loves' PLTR as Iran War Rages On
recentNews.significance: Barchart: Rare case of software stock rallying on geopolitical conflict. Ontology platform expected to maintain demand even post-conflict.

recentNews.headline: Institutional Ownership Surges from 7% to 32%
recentNews.significance: GuruFocus: 4.6x increase in institutional ownership over one year. Structural shift from retail to institutional demand.

recentNews.headline: Seeking Alpha: 'Hard to Sell a Company That Benefits from Geopolitical Conflicts'
recentNews.significance: SaaS scalability ($4.4B revenue with minimal infrastructure) + accelerating revenue and earnings growth. Structural Strong Buy thesis.

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: 'Up 2,200% Since 2023 — Can the Run Continue?'
recentNews.significance: Valuation is expensive but years of rapid growth could justify the price. Balanced analysis of both sides.

recentNews.headline: +4.72% Surge on Iran Tensions — Starts +15% Weekly Rally
recentNews.significance: US-Iran military conflict triggered defense AI demand expectations. CNBC: 'PLTR +15% weekly, exceptional for software stocks.'

analystOpinions.notableComment: 16 of 28 analysts rate Buy. 24/7 Wall St conservative at $161.91. Wedbush Dan Ives calls it 'garage sale prices.' Iran war tailwind and institutional demand increase support further upside.

risks.title: Extreme Valuation — P/E ~238x
risks.description: Forward P/E 114x is the highest in AI sector. If Iran war tailwind proves temporary, premium could contract sharply.

risks.title: Ongoing Insider Selling
risks.description: CEO Karp + Director Thiel recently sold $400M+. Institutional ownership increase partially offsets, but continued selling creates pressure.

risks.title: Iran War Resolution = 'Tailwind Reversal' Risk
risks.description: President Trump suggesting war could end 'soon.' Actual resolution could reduce defense premium.

risks.title: Government Budget Dependency ~55%
risks.description: Vulnerable to budget cuts, administration changes, and policy shifts. Growing commercial mix is key.

risks.title: Share Dilution +4.67% YoY
risks.description: Stock-based compensation increasing share count. Long-term EPS dilution effect.

buyReasons.title: Iran War + Institutional Demand Surge = Structural Revaluation
buyReasons.rationale: Defense AI demand surge + institutional ownership 7%→32%. Transcending temporary events into structural positioning shift.

buyReasons.title: Revenue +70% YoY + FCF Margin 47% — Growth and Profitability Together
buyReasons.rationale: SaaS leverage generating $4.4B revenue with minimal infrastructure. Net cash $6.95B.

buyReasons.title: Ontology Platform — Unique AI-Human Collaboration Moat
buyReasons.rationale: Positioned as a 'tool factory' beyond simple data analytics. Competitive edge in both battlefield and enterprise AI.

buyReasons.title: Above 50-day SMA ($146.33) — Technical Rebound Underway
buyReasons.rationale: Iran rally recovered 50-day SMA. If $150 support holds, additional upside momentum likely.

overallOpinion: Palantir closed Wednesday at $150.14 (-0.66%), a slight pullback after a +15% weekly rally on Iran war tailwinds.

The most notable development is institutional ownership surging 4.6x from 7% to 32% over the past year. This structural shift from retail to institutional investors improves price stability and upside potential.

The challenge remains valuation: P/E ~238x, Forward P/E ~114x. If the Iran war resolves, the defense premium could shrink, leaving the age-old question of 'great company, expensive stock.' Holding above 50-day SMA ($146.33), with 200-day SMA ($162.38) recovery as the next target.

sources.name: Barchart
sources.description: Wall Street loves PLTR amid Iran war

threads.hook: PLTR institutional ownership 7%→32% 📈 Smart money buying
threads.text: Iran war drove +15% weekly rally. But the bigger story: institutional ownership surged 4.6x in a year. Ontology platform is irreplaceable in battlefield AI. P/E 238x is the elephant in the room though. What would you do? 👉 investory.kro.kr/stock/PLTR/analysis/2026-03-12

telegram.hook: ⚔️ PLTR $150.14 (-0.66%) — Iran war boost + 4.6x institutional ownership surge
telegram.text:
Small pullback after +15% weekly Iran war rally. The real news: institutional ownership jumped from 7% to 32% in one year.

Wall Street analysis: Rare case of a software stock rallying on geopolitics. Ontology 'tool factory' expected to maintain defense contracts post-conflict.

Seeking Alpha: $4.4B revenue on minimal infrastructure, SaaS scalability. Revenue +70%, FCF margin 47%.

P/E 238x is the only concern. Iran war resolution = potential premium compression.
